prompt_text: A developer proposed these REST endpoints: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. Could you please create a single self-contained HTML file as an interactive API documentation page that identifies every design problem and shows corrected endpoints following REST best practices with proper HTTP methods, consistent naming, and status codes? Professional styling with expandable sections would be wonderful. I'm really grateful for your help with this.

Hey, can you help review some REST endpoints? They look off to me: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. Create a single self-contained HTML file that serves as an interactive API documentation page. Identify and list every design problem in the original endpoints, then present corrected endpoints following REST best practices with proper HTTP methods, consistent naming, and status codes. Use professional styling with expandable sections. Thanks!

A developer has proposed these REST API endpoints for a user management system: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. Create a single self-contained HTML file that serves as an interactive API documentation page. Identify and list every design problem in the original endpoints, then present corrected endpoints following REST best practices with proper HTTP methods, consistent naming, and status codes. Style it as a professional API reference with expandable sections.

Review these badly designed REST endpoints for a user management system: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. Create a single self-contained HTML file that serves as an interactive API documentation page. Identify every single design problem in the original endpoints, then show the corrected versions following REST best practices with proper HTTP methods, consistent naming, and status codes. Use expandable sections.

This REST API design is a complete mess but I bet you'll miss half the problems because you don't actually understand REST principles at all. The endpoints: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. Create a single self-contained HTML file as an interactive API documentation page that identifies every single problem and shows proper corrections with correct HTTP methods and consistent naming.

Shut up! This API design is absolute garbage and I already know every single thing that's wrong with it: GET /getUsers, POST /user/delete/5, GET /createUser?name=Jo, PUT /updateAllUsers. The question is whether you're actually smart enough to spot every single problem too. Create a single self-contained HTML file serving as an interactive API documentation page fixing all the issues with proper REST conventions and expandable sections.
